howItWorks.intro: This boxing reach calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Reach = Wingspan ÷ 2 (arm length from center chest)
Reach Advantage = Your Wingspan - Opponent Wingspan
Positive ape index = longer arms relative to height
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is the ape index?
faq.answer: Ape index is your wingspan minus your height. A positive ape index means your arms are longer than you are tall — an advantage in boxing, climbing, and swimming. The average is 0 (wingspan equals height).

faq.question: How important is reach in boxing?
faq.answer: Reach is one of the most significant physical advantages. Fighters with longer reach can hit opponents while staying out of range. However, shorter-armed fighters can overcome this with head movement and inside fighting.

faq.question: What is the average boxing reach?
faq.answer: Average reach is approximately equal to height. Professional boxers average about 71-73 inches at middleweight. Notable long reaches include Tyson Fury (85") and Thomas Hearns (78").
